topic: surge pricing strategy — when to drive vs when to wait

turns.content: question for you. when surge hits on lyft do you think its better to drive straight into it or wait on the edge for a better ping

It depends on the situation. If you're already close to the surge zone, driving in makes sense because you'll likely get matched fast. But if you're 10-15 minutes away, the surge might die down by the time you arrive. Waiting on the edge of a surge zone can sometimes land you a ride from someone trying to escape the higher-price area. What's your usual move?
i usually chase it bro and half the time it disappears by the time i get there. its so frustrating

That's super common. Surge chasing is one of the biggest traps in rideshare. The algorithm adjusts fast — by the time you drive 10 minutes toward a surge zone, enough drivers have flooded in and it drops. A better play is to learn the patterns. Like if you know a certain area surges every Friday at 11pm, position yourself there at 10:45 instead of reacting after it pops.
thats smart. like downtown houston always surges after the astros games or any big event. i should just be there early

Exactly. Event-based surges are the most predictable ones. Astros games, Texans games, concerts at Toyota Center — you can literally check the schedule and plan your week. Park nearby 20-30 minutes before the event ends and you're golden. Airport surges are also pretty predictable if you track flight schedules.
the airport is hit or miss tho. sometimes you wait in that lot for 45 minutes for one ride

Yeah, the airport queue can be a time killer. The math usually doesn't work out unless there's a big surge multiplier. Sitting in a lot for 45 minutes earning nothing versus doing two or three shorter rides in that time — the shorter rides usually win. The only time airport runs make sense is late at night when the surge is high and there's less competition in the queue.
facts bro. late night airport is where the money is. like 1am 2am when international flights land and theres barely any drivers

That's a sweet spot for sure. Fewer drivers, tired travelers who just want to get home and won't argue about surge pricing, and usually longer rides too since they're going to suburbs or hotels. If you can handle the late hours, that 1-2am window at IAH is probably one of the most efficient times to drive.
yeah i been doing that more lately. the only problem is im dead the next day lol. but the money is worth it

The tradeoff is real. One thing some drivers do is split their day — work a morning shift, take a break in the afternoon, then come back for the late-night surge window. Keeps you from burning out on one long stretch. But I get it, sometimes you just gotta push through and make the money when it's there.
do you think running both uber and lyft at the same time helps with surge or nah

Running both apps at the same time can definitely help. If one app isn't surging, the other might be. You can cherry-pick the higher-paying requests. Just make sure to go offline on one as soon as you accept a ride on the other — you don't want to miss pickups or tank your acceptance rate on either platform.

yeah bro thats basically what i do. morning rush then break then late night. and i toggle between both apps. its a grind but it works. appreciate the tips on the surge thing tho ima stop chasing and start positioning
That mindset shift from chasing to positioning will save you a lot of wasted miles and frustration. Check event schedules at the start of each week and plan around them. Good luck out there, man.
